extraction.one_line_summary: VAN DER POEL Mathieu attacked repeatedly in final 40km, dropped on Cauberg with 4km to go, regained contact in tailwind finale, won from small group sprint
tactical_patterns: Van der Poel's repeated attacks from 38km out forced selection but left him vulnerable to counterattacks on Cauberg
Kwiatkowski's solo move stuck briefly because marked riders (Alaphilippe, Fuglsang) hesitated watching each other instead of chasing
Tailwind final kilometer allowed dropped riders to rejoin, nullifying Cauberg selections - finish positioning trumped climbing strength
Van der Poel sprinted from small group after being dropped, suggesting superior recovery from early attacks

rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: positive: won despite being dropped on Cauberg 4km out, exceptional recovery ability makes him dangerous even after appearing cooked
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michal: negative: got clear inside 5km but couldn't hold off regrouping behind, solo strength insufficient at race-critical moment
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: marked on Cauberg but failed to respond decisively to Kwiatkowski's move, hesitation cost podium opportunity
rider_signals.FUGLSANG Jakob: neutral: strong enough for podium group but tactical hesitation with Alaphilippe allowed Kwiatkowski's brief escape
rider_signals.CLARKE Simon: positive: second place from small group without making headline attacks, positioning discipline in finale more effective than flashy moves
